items.title: US Initial Jobless Claims Fall to Lowest Level Since 1969
items.description: First-time applications for US unemployment benefits fell last week to the lowest level since 1969, signaling layoffs remain muted in a stable labor market.

items.title: The Most Fundamental Macro Risk Is Back: MacroScope
items.description: Rising inflation volatility is set to trigger earnings disappointments, wider credit spreads and more variability in stock and bond prices.

items.title: Brent Crude Hits $100 a Barrel on Red Sea, Iran Supply Threats
items.description: Brent crude hit $100 a barrel for the first time in two months after Iran-backed Houthi militants said they attacked two Saudi Arabian tankers in the Red Sea, escalating the Middle East conflict and t

items.title: US Oil Exports Set for a Comeback as Renewed Iran Conflict Spurs Buyers
items.description: Further disruption to Middle East shipments prompts Asia and Europe to look elsewhere for supplies.

items.title: US Treasury Yields Rise to 2026 Highs as Oil Gains Spark Fed Bet
items.description: US Treasury yields rose to their highest levels of the year as the threat of escalation in the Iran war sent oil prices higher, boosting expectations that the Federal Reserve could raise interest rate

items.title: India Seeks to Expand Investment Options for Portfolio Managers
items.description: India’s markets regulator has proposed allowing portfolio managers to invest clients’ money in overseas securities and IPO-bound firms, as well as take unhedged equity derivatives positions, as part o

items.title: US Stock Futures Drop as Traders Focus on AI Spending, Oil Jumps
items.description: US stock futures retreated as worries over artificial-intelligence spending weighed on sentiment while oil prices closed in on $100 a barrel. Traders braced for more key earnings from Intel Corp., due

items.title: Cellnex Studies Strategic Options After Share Price Slump
items.description: Cellnex Telecom SA is evaluating strategic options, according to people familiar with the matter, as the wireless tower operator looks at ways to grapple with its debt load and weak share price.

items.title: GCM Grosvenor Raises $1.2 Billion for Credit Secondaries Push
items.description: GCM Grosvenor has raised $1.2 billion for its first dedicated fund strategy targeting credit secondaries.

items.title: Alphabet’s Century Bond Has Now Lost a 10th of Its Face Value
items.description: A 100-year bond that Alphabet Inc. issued earlier this year in the sterling market as it gorged on debt around the world has dropped below 90 pence on the pound for the first time.

items.title: India May Allow Portfolio Managers to Take Unhedged Short Bets
items.description: India’s securities regulator has proposed allowing domestic portfolio management service providers to take short positions in equity derivatives for the first time, easing rules for professional money

items.title: JPMorgan Quants Say Strongest Earnings Upgrades Are in Europe
items.description: Europe now leads the world as the region with the sharpest upgrades to corporate earnings estimates from analysts, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co.

items.title: Kuehne+Nagel Weighs Options For Apex Logistics Unit
items.description: Kuehne+Nagel International AG is considering strategic options for its Apex Logistics unit, an Asia-focused freight forwarder, people with knowledge of the matter said.

items.title: McCormick Will Add London Listing After Unilever Food Deal
items.description: McCormick & Co. has picked London as a secondary listing location, just months after striking a deal to combine with Unilever Plc’s food business and in a blow to Amsterdam’s hopes.

items.title: Asian Oil Buyers in Talks to Reroute Saudi Red Sea Flows
items.description: Some Asian crude oil buyers are in talks with Saudi Aramco to potentially reroute flows around Africa after Houthi attacks on tankers in the Red Sea, according to traders familiar with the matter.

items.title: BlackRock Says Treasury Yields Offer Buffer Against Losses
items.description: US Treasuries are offering investors solid protection against losses as yields remain elevated, according to BlackRock Inc.

items.title: Airtel Mobile-Money Unit Picks London for Listing Location
items.description: Airtel Africa Plc picked London as the primary listing location for its mobile-money business and expects to start trading there this year, confirming an earlier report by Bloomberg.

items.title: Crop Prices Hit 3-Year High as Black Sea Attacks, Heat Stoke Food Inflation
items.description: Crop prices rose to a three-year high as heat waves and escalating attacks in the Black Sea threatened to disrupt global grain trade, reviving risks to food inflation that have also been stoked by the

items.title: Stocks Fall on AI Jitters as Oil Lifts Bond Yields: Markets Wrap
items.description: Stocks fell as Alphabet Inc.’s earnings triggered fresh unease over ever-higher artificial intelligence spending, while rising oil prices drove bond yields higher.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 23
